misc.title: The “Omen” feels familiar to me. In the Elbaph mural, there’s this demon-like figure reaching out its hand, and at the end of that reach is a mysterious figure with wings and something like a halo. Th
misc.text: The “Omen” feels familiar to me. In the Elbaph mural, there’s this demon-like figure reaching out its hand, and at the end of that reach is a mysterious figure with wings and something like a halo. That halo-like object could very well be the original form of what we now see as the “omen.” It almost feels like that halo was taken by the demon, causing the white to turn black—eventually becoming the omen as it is now. It’s basically portrayed like a fallen angel.

misc.text: Aunque lo más sorprendente de todo (y que ya se intuía al final del capítulo anterior) es el ENORME parecido que hay entre “Imu” y “Enel” 😲😲😲 ¿Estarán relacionados de alguna forma? Por cierto, espero que veamos a Enel de nuevo en el Manga en un futuro, me encanta el personaje.

misc.title: ¡¡ÉPICO CAPÍTULO EL #OnePiece1180 😍😍😍!! Adoro el diseño de Imu, y su superioridad es aplastante. Respecto a sus poderes, es muy interesante que los kanji de “Omen” (魔気 que pueden leerse como “Maki”
misc.text: ¡¡ÉPICO CAPÍTULO EL #OnePiece1180 😍😍😍!! Adoro el diseño de Imu, y su superioridad es aplastante. Respecto a sus poderes, es muy interesante que los kanji de “Omen” (魔気 que pueden leerse como “Maki”) sean tan parecidos a los de “Haki” (覇気). ¿Será su contraparte “demoníaca”? items.title: The “Omen” feels familiar to me. In the Elbaph mural, there’s this demon-like figure reaching out its hand, and at the end of that reach is a mysterious figure with wings and something like a halo. Th
items.text: The “Omen” feels familiar to me. In the Elbaph mural, there’s this demon-like figure reaching out its hand, and at the end of that reach is a mysterious figure with wings and something like a halo. That halo-like object could very well be the original form of what we now see as the “omen.” It almost feels like that halo was taken by the demon, causing the white to turn black—eventually becoming the omen as it is now. It’s basically portrayed like a fallen angel.
